governing_requirement: Bind every consumed bridge artifact to actual encoded bytes and canonical decoded pixels plus decoder/media/owner/ontology/provider/runtime/authority/QA/certificate/lineage/transform/revocation evidence; missing, ambiguous, stale or drifted evidence must prevent consumption and cache reuse.

dependency_state.disposition: MF-P6-09.04 remains dependency and implementation blocked; only present declared-binding foundations receive credit.

prior_evidence.disposition: Accepted for its field map, missing-proof inventory and additive packet. Prerequisite states are superseded by the current tracker.

verified_foundations: Frozen request/receipt/certificate schemas require encoded/decoded source and output identities, decoder/orientation/color/value semantics, media time, subject/assignment, target/protected inputs, provider stack, route/resources, artifacts, transforms, QA, authority, lineage, eligibility, trust and signed canonical hashes.

Exchange validation binds the exact request/receipt IDs/body, media, source, subject, assignment, release/capability, transform, target/protected lineage, one output per intent, owner, package lineage, resource envelope and certificate scope.
Receipt validation recomputes declared artifact semantic identities and binds outputs to source decoded pixels, transform, intent, owner and lineage.
Production exchange validation can compare exact supplied release/capability/certificate documents and fresh revocation state; release-bundle validation rejects unmanifested release artifacts.
Operational-certificate issuance has actual source/output byte checks, but that issuer-specific gate is not a general bridge consumption/cache decision.
Four focused transaction/ownership/receipt/release-closure tests pass and Ruff passes; all governing source hashes match the accepted prior audit.

direct_probes.mutation: Change one provider model artifact SHA, retain stack_sha256/execution_fingerprint_sha256 and trusted re-sign the receipt.
direct_probes.gap: Provider stack and execution fingerprint are asserted rather than recomputed from components.

direct_probes.mutation: Change output encoded/decoded hashes, recompute the semantic artifact identity and all internal identity references, then trusted re-sign without supplying any file.
direct_probes.gap: Document consistency passes without resolving actual encoded or decoded artifact bytes.

direct_probes.mutation: Change qa.report_sha256 and trusted re-sign without supplying report bytes.
direct_probes.gap: QA evidence identity is asserted at consumption time.
No canonical cache key/freshness/tombstone decision or general actual-byte consumption gate exists.

confirmed_gaps: No general governed-root consumer resolves every source/output/QA/selection-evidence path and rejects escape, link/reparse, case-alias, special-file, missing and unmanifested states.
No consumption decision reads actual encoded bytes and canonical decoded pixels/masks and recomputes decoder/orientation/color/alpha/dtype/value/dimension/content facts.
Provider stack and execution fingerprints are not recomputed from exact model, workflow, runtime, environment, route and execution components.
QA report and selection-evidence hashes are not byte-resolved at consumption time.
No canonical cache identity includes release, capability, request, media/source, ontology, policy, provider, artifact, transform, QA, certificate, lineage and revocation state.
No freshness decision rechecks certificate/release/provider/ontology/policy/revocation state before reuse or emits a typed tombstone/no-consume decision.
No strict signed decision record binds normalized actual evidence, cache key/freshness, allow/reject result and deterministic reasons.

implementation_packet.path_state: All four allowed paths remain absent, untracked and clean; they are disjoint from frozen v1, dirty 08.05 and prepared 09.01/09.02 paths.
implementation_packet.next_action: Hold until MF-P6-09.01/09.02 are implemented/adopted through a qualified exact-project path; do not dispatch or duplicate while worker lanes remain disabled.

claim_boundary: This audit credits strict declared transaction/artifact/certificate binding only. It does not verify actual consumed bytes, recompute provider/QA evidence, authorize cache reuse, implement freshness/tombstone behavior, satisfy MF-P6-09.01/09.02, or complete MF-P6-09.04.
